findings.title: Unchecked ERC20 arithmetic lets arbitrary callers drain approved-none balances

best_agent_match.title: Unchecked allowance arithmetic lets anyone steal arbitrary ERC20 balances

findings.title: Transfer gate becomes a honeypot by permanently blocking sells to the chosen pool address after 50 blocks

best_agent_match.title: Hidden honeypot permanently blocks sells to the pool after 50 blocks

findings.title: Buyers only get a two-block window to sell back to the pool

best_agent_match.title: Hidden honeypot permanently blocks sells to the pool after 50 blocks

rejected_candidates.title: safeTransferFrom lacks authorization verification
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. Both `safeTransferFrom` overloads delegate to `transferFrom`, and the NFT branch of `transferFrom` enforces owner/approval/operator authorization before moving the token.

rejected_candidates.title: Ownership revocation permanently locks contract
rejected_candidates.reason: `revokeOwnership()` is an intentional renounce-ownership pattern. It may freeze admin functionality, but that is not a protocol vulnerability or user-funds issue by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero address validation in setWhitelist
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-only configuration issue with no realistic standalone exploit path or protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Burn/mint loops may exceed gas limits for large transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: Under normal operation the native supply is only 200 tokens, so the burn/mint loops are bounded. Pathological huge-loop behavior depends on the separate unchecked-arithmetic bug already captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: ownerOf reverts instead of returning address(0) for non-existent IDs
rejected_candidates.reason: False premise. Standard ERC721 `ownerOf` behavior is to revert for nonexistent tokens, not return `address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unverified token allocation to owner at deployment
rejected_candidates.reason: Initial token allocation/distribution policy is a project design choice, not a smart-contract vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Monotonic minted counter causes permanent ERC20/ERC721 type confusion
rejected_candidates.reason: Code support exists, but the misclassification threshold is measured in raw 18-decimal base units. At this 200-token scale it only affects dust-sized amounts, making the impact too weak to keep as a reportable protocol issue.
